message:
🌱 Add keycloak: locale attribute not set after registration mission (#996)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-13505-locale-attribute-not-set-after-registration",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: locale attribute not set after registration",
+    "description": "When a user creates a new account using the registration link, the user's locale is not saved as an attribute. This causes localization issues for subsequent emails and UI interactions because Keycloak falls back to the Accept-Language header instead of using the user's selected locale. This issue affects 22+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the locale attribute issue",
+        "description": "Check if newly registered users are missing the locale attribute:\n```bash\n# Check your Keycloak version:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n\n# List user attributes for a recently registered user:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users \\\n  -r myrealm --query username=newuser --fields 'attributes'\n```\nIf the `locale` attribute is missing or empty for newly registered users, you are affected."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the root cause",
+        "description": "Keycloak's `LocaleSelectorProvider` used the browser's `Accept-Language` header to resolve the locale for all contexts, including email themes. During registration, the user's selected locale was not being persisted as a user attribute.\n\nThe fix adds a new method to `KeycloakContext` and `LocaleSelectorProvider` that accepts a theme type parameter, preventing the use of the `Accept-Language` header when resolving the locale for email themes."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade to the fixed version",
+        "description": "Upgrade your Keycloak installation to a version that includes the fix from [PR #30397](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/30397):\n```bash\n# Example: upgrade the Keycloak container\ndocker pull quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest\ndocker stop keycloak && docker rm keycloak\ndocker run -d --name keycloak \\\n  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin \\\n  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin \\\n  -p 8080:8080 \\\n  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest start-dev\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm locale attribute is set after registration",
+        "description": "Verify the fix:\n1. Register a new user while the browser is set to a non-default locale\n2. Check the user's attributes in the Admin Console or via the API\n3. Confirm the `locale` attribute is correctly set to the user's selected locale\n4. Trigger an email (e.g., password reset) and confirm it arrives in the correct locale"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The fix adds a new locale resolution method to KeycloakContext and LocaleSelectorProvider that accepts a theme type parameter. This prevents the use of the Accept-Language header when email themes are used, ensuring the user's selected locale is correctly persisted as a user attribute during registration.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/13505",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/30397"
+    },
+    "reactions": 22,
+    "comments": 6,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes) with Admin Console access."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:49:01.071Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,

+  }
+}
